slides.title: Vaccines are *training* for an aging immune system.
slides.sub: Age-appropriate shots like shingles and pneumococcal can prepare your defenses before an infection ever arrives.

slides.title: Immunity fades with age
slides.body: After about 50, immune cells respond *more slowly*. Vaccines give the body a rehearsal so it can react fast when it matters.

slides.title: Practice before the threat
slides.body: A shot teaches your immune system to recognize a pathogen in advance, so a real exposure meets a defense that is *already ready*.

slides.title: Nerve pain you can avoid
slides.body: Shingles can leave lasting nerve pain that lingers for months. The vaccine *is linked to* far fewer cases in older adults.

slides.title: Fewer hospital stays
slides.body: Pneumococcal and flu shots can lower the odds of pneumonia and hospital stays that often *trigger lasting decline*.

slides.title: Protecting your momentum
slides.body: Avoiding a serious infection keeps you moving, sleeping and eating well. One illness can undo *months of progress*.

slides.title: Stay well enough to *keep going*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food, movement and steps that build resilience, and scores them in one daily Health Score so you notice when your body needs rest.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The best time to defend your health is before you need to. Age-appropriate vaccines train an aging immune system in advance. 🌿💉

Shingles, pneumococcal and flu shots can mean less nerve pain, fewer hospital stays and more of the good years. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Adult Vaccines Are Training for Your Immune System
